name: Marcilla, Peralta, San Adrián, Calahorra, Funes – Circular Route
name_original: Marcilla, Peralta, San Adrián, Calahorra, Funes – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Touring cycling around Marcilla offers routes through varied landscapes in the province of Navarre, Spain. The region features flat terrain and gentle rolling hills, providing accessible cycling experiences. Cyclists can explore agricultural paths and plains, with opportunities to reach more diverse environments like the semi-desert Bardenas Reales or forested areas of northern Navarre. The area's geography allows for routes that combine natural beauty with historical sites. Best touring cycling routes around Marcilla The most popular touring cycling route is Marcilla, Peralta, San Adrián, Calahorra, Funes – Circular Route, a 30.4 miles (49.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route connects several towns across the plains and rolling hills. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Funes – Peralta loop from Funes, a difficult 21.2 miles (34.1 km) path. This route offers a challenging ride through the region's characteristic flat and gently undulating terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Marcilla Castle – Old Town of Peralta loop from Falces, a 24.3 miles (39.0 km) trail leading through agricultural paths and past historical landmarks, often completed in about 3 hours 21 minutes. Touring cycling around Marcilla is defined by flat plains, gentle rolling hills, and agricultural paths, with proximity to diverse Navarrese landscapes like the Bardenas Reales. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult excursions. The routes in Marcilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Marcilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Marcilla?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Marcilla, offering a variety of experiences through the region's landscapes. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes for all skill levels in Marcilla?
A: Yes, Marcilla offers touring cycling routes suitable for various skill levels. While many routes feature flat terrain and gentle rolling hills, there are options ranging from 1 easy route to 16 moderate and 6 difficult routes, ensuring a suitable challenge for everyone.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Marcilla?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Marcilla are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975524463/marcilla-peralta-san-adrian-calahorra-funes-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla, Peralta, San Adrián, Calahorra, Funes – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which connects several towns across the plains and rolling hills.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling near Marcilla?
A: Touring cycling around Marcilla primarily features routes through green fields, large plains, and gentle rolling hills, characteristic of the Navarre region. You'll also find agricultural paths and can reach more diverse environments like the semi-desert Bardenas Reales or forested areas of northern Navarre on longer excursions.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions to visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2210948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which is often integrated into routes. Other nearby attractions include the Royal Palace of Olite, and various medieval villages that dot the landscape, offering cultural stops during your rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Marcilla?
A: Marcilla generally offers favorable terrain for cycling. The region's diverse geography means you can enjoy routes through plains and rolling hills. While specific weather conditions vary, the mild climate of Navarre often makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the varied landscapes of Navarre provide several opportunities for scenic views. For instance, you can find routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalén Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or offer views of the confluence of the Arga and Aragón rivers, providing picturesque stops during your tour.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with exploring nearby towns or natural reserves?
A: Absolutely. Marcilla's location allows for routes that connect to other towns like Peralta, Funes, and San Adrián. You can also explore natural areas such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4047253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Pitillas Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, or venture further to the unique landscapes of the Bardenas Reales.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Marcilla?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Marcilla, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the accessible routes through flat plains and gentle rolling hills, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites like Marcilla Castle, and the opportunity to explore diverse Navarrese landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the Marcilla area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are several difficult touring cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24551690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Funes – Peralta loop from Funes&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.2-mile (34.1 km) path that offers a challenging ride through the region's characteristic terrain.

Q: Where can I find a touring cycling route that includes historical sites?
A: A great option for combining cycling with history is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24552250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcilla Castle – Old Town of Peralta loop from Falces&lt;/a&gt;. This 24.3-mile (39.0 km) route leads through agricultural paths and past significant historical landmarks, including Marcilla Castle.

Q: Is it possible to find longer touring cycling routes from Marcilla?
A: Yes, you can embark on longer excursions from Marcilla. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martin de Unx – Olite Royal Palace loop from Caparroso&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.9-mile (72.2 km) route that takes you through varied landscapes and past the Royal Palace of Olite.
tips:
It is located at Plaza de la Cava, 1, in the town center of Marcilla (Navarra), easily accessible and surrounded by bars and a children's playground.
This 15th-century Gothic fortress was built of brick on a stone foundation by Mosén Pierres de Peralta, with material and financial support from Charles III the Noble.
It served both as a defensive stronghold and as a palatial residence for the Marquesses of Falces, descendants of Peralta.
In 1516, the Marchioness Ana de Velasco saved the castle from being demolished by order of Cardinal Cisneros.
A small town of about 6,000 people. In its old town there are various palaces and houses from the Baroque era, with beautiful facades.
Excellent place to eat and spend a full day of cultural visits. A labyrinth of streets and humble houses as used by the Romans and Jews who lived here for more than five centuries.
